refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it requires explicit definition of: (1) the measurement period and baseline, (2) what constitutes 'available' vs. 'unavailable' (e.g., does safety shutdown count as unavailable?), (3) the scope of the system (vehicle only, or full NGHE solution including teleoperation and support services?), and (4) the role of planned vs. unplanned downtime. High safety criticality demands that safety-driven shutdowns are tracked separately from availability failures.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Collect operational logs from deployed NGHE units over the agreed evaluation period (e.g., 90 days, 6 months, 1 year). Calculate availability as (total scheduled time − unplanned downtime − safety-driven shutdowns) / total scheduled time. Compare against the ≥0.97 threshold. Stratify by failure mode (mechanical, electrical, software, safety intervention) to identify root causes and improvement opportunities.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall operate without unplanned failure for a mean time between failures (MTBF) of at least X hours.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain network connectivity and command responsiveness with ≥0.99 uptime during scheduled operational windows.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall detect and respond to hazardous conditions without introducing false positives that exceed Y% of operational time.
FR-NGHE-004.4: Planned maintenance shall be scheduled and executed such that it does not exceed Z% of total scheduled operational time.
FR-NGHE-004.5: The NGHE solution shall provide diagnostic and predictive maintenance capabilities to minimize unplanned downtime.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundancy and fault tolerance across all critical subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) with automated failover and health monitoring.
DP-B: Design modular, replaceable components with rapid swap-out procedures and maintain spare inventory to minimize mean time to repair (MTTR).
DP-C: Establish a predictive maintenance program using telemetry data analysis to identify degradation early and schedule maintenance proactively.
DP-D: Implement a comprehensive availability management system that tracks uptime metrics, root causes, and triggers corrective actions when availability falls below target.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A + DP-C + DP-D (integrated approach): Implement redundancy and fault tolerance across critical subsystems with automated failover; establish predictive maintenance using telemetry analysis; deploy an availability management system that monitors KPI in real-time and triggers corrective actions. This multi-faceted approach addresses both prevention (redundancy, predictive maintenance) and detection/response (monitoring, corrective action).

potential_sources: Redundancy in Vehicle subsystem (e.g., dual hydraulic systems) may couple with Teleoperation control logic if failover is not transparent to the operator.
Predictive maintenance algorithms depend on Telemetry interface quality; poor data quality will degrade prediction accuracy.
Availability management system must integrate with Safety Services to avoid counting safety-driven shutdowns as failures.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP is not orthogonal but is justified because availability is inherently a system-level property that requires coordinated action across multiple subsystems. Coupling is manageable through clear interface contracts (Telemetry data schema, SafetyAlert semantics, failover transparency requirements).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Redundancy can be implemented cost-effectively without exceeding budget constraints.
Assumption: Telemetry data is sufficiently rich and reliable to enable predictive maintenance.
Risk: Over-reliance on redundancy may mask underlying design flaws; root cause analysis is essential.
Risk: Predictive maintenance algorithms may have false positive/negative rates that impact availability perception.
Risk: Availability management system introduces additional complexity and potential failure modes.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to specify which subsystems require redundancy (e.g., critical path items only) and the redundancy strategy (active-active, active-passive, N+1). Refine DP-C to define the predictive maintenance algorithm, data requirements, and acceptable false positive rate. Refine DP-D to specify the availability KPI calculation method, monitoring frequency, and corrective action triggers.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems to deliver the overall availability KPI. It owns the availability management system, predictive maintenance coordination, and cross-subsystem health monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems, including operational status, fault codes, performance metrics, and environmental data. Used by predictive maintenance and availability monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Inbound safety alerts from Safety Services subsystem. Used to distinguish safety-driven shutdowns from availability failures.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outbound tasking commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems, including maintenance scheduling and failover directives.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional network connectivity for remote monitoring, diagnostics, and software updates to support availability management.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of the availability KPI and the orchestration logic. Availability is a system-level property that requires integration across all subsystems. Minimal diffusion risk because the availability management system is logically cohesive.

Vehicle owns mechanical and electrical reliability but does not own Teleoperation or Safety Services. Assigning availability KPI to Vehicle alone would fragment the requirement and obscure cross-subsystem dependencies.
Teleoperation owns network connectivity and command responsiveness but does not own Vehicle reliability or Safety Services. Assigning availability KPI to Teleoperation alone would be incomplete.

Safety Services owns hazard detection and response but does not own Vehicle or Teleoperation reliability. Assigning availability KPI to Safety Services alone would be incomplete and could create perverse incentives (e.g., suppressing safety alerts to improve availability).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of the availability KPI because: (1) availability is a system-level emergent property that depends on the coordinated operation of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (2) the availability management system (monitoring, predictive maintenance, corrective action) is logically cohesive and should be centralized to avoid diffusion; (3) the NGHE Solution module has visibility into all subsystems via Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces; (4) ownership at the NGHE Solution level enables clear accountability and prevents finger-pointing between subsystems.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may become a bottleneck if availability management logic is overly centralized. Mitigation: Use event-driven architecture and asynchronous communication to distribute processing.
Risk: Availability management system may introduce latency in failover or corrective action. Mitigation: Define strict latency budgets and test failover scenarios.
Risk: Telemetry data quality issues may propagate to availability calculations. Mitigation: Implement data validation and quality checks at the Telemetry interface.
Risk: Safety Services may suppress alerts to improve availability metrics. Mitigation: Implement separate tracking of safety-driven shutdowns and establish safety as a non-negotiable constraint.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define the internal architecture of the NGHE Solution module to specify: (1) the availability monitoring component (real-time KPI calculation), (2) the predictive maintenance component (algorithm, data pipeline), (3) the corrective action component (failover logic, maintenance scheduling), and (4) the interfaces between these components. Establish clear ownership boundaries between NGHE Solution and its subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) to avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundancy + predictive maintenance + availability management) directly addresses the FR (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP is comprehensive and accounts for both prevention (redundancy, predictive maintenance) and detection/response (monitoring, corrective action). The DP is feasible given current technology and industry best practices. No fundamental mismatch between FR and DP.

diffusion_risks: Redundancy logic may be distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems, creating diffusion. Mitigation: Define a centralized failover orchestration layer in NGHE Solution.

Predictive maintenance algorithms may be implemented in multiple subsystems (e.g., Vehicle diagnostics, Teleoperation network monitoring). Mitigation: Establish a centralized predictive maintenance pipeline in NGHE Solution that aggregates data from all subsystems.
Availability monitoring may be duplicated across subsystems. Mitigation: Implement a single source of truth for availability KPI in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate container for the availability management system. However, the implementation of redundancy and predictive maintenance will require coordination with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Clear interface contracts (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking) are essential to manage this coordination.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the availability KPI is feasible through operational testing and field deployment. Availability can be measured directly from operational logs. Root cause analysis can be performed on failures to identify improvement opportunities. Predictive maintenance effectiveness can be validated by comparing predicted failures to actual failures.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the availability KPI to the NGHE Solution module is clear. Sub-FRs (MTBF for Vehicle, uptime for Teleoperation, false positive rate for Safety Services, maintenance scheduling) can be allocated to respective subsystems. The NGHE Solution module owns the integration and orchestration logic.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to specify the redundancy strategy (active-active, active-passive, N+1) and which subsystems require redundancy.
Refine the DP to specify the predictive maintenance algorithm, data requirements, and acceptable false positive/negative rates.
Refine the DP to specify the availability KPI calculation method, monitoring frequency, and corrective action triggers.
Define the internal architecture of the NGHE Solution module to specify the availability monitoring, predictive maintenance, and corrective action components.
Establish clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and its subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) to manage coordination and avoid diffusion.

decision.justification: The refined FR is not atomic and has been decomposed into five sub-FRs (MTBF for Vehicle, uptime for Teleoperation, false positive rate for Safety Services, maintenance scheduling, and predictive maintenance). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define specific design parameters and implementation modules. The selected DP is comprehensive but requires refinement to specify redundancy strategy, predictive maintenance algorithm, and availability monitoring logic. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate but requires definition of internal architecture and component boundaries. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to allocate requirements to subsystems and define detailed design parameters.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall operate without unplanned failure for a mean time between failures (MTBF) of at least X hours.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain network connectivity and command responsiveness with ≥0.99 uptime during scheduled operational windows.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall detect and respond to hazardous conditions without introducing false positives that exceed Y% of operational time.
FR-NGHE-004.4: Planned maintenance shall be scheduled and executed such that it does not exceed Z% of total scheduled operational time.
FR-NGHE-004.5: The NGHE solution shall provide diagnostic and predictive maintenance capabilities to minimize unplanned downtime.

assumptions: Availability is measured as (total scheduled time − unplanned downtime − safety-driven shutdowns) / total scheduled time.
Safety-driven shutdowns are tracked separately and do not count against availability.
Redundancy can be implemented cost-effectively without exceeding budget constraints.
Telemetry data is sufficiently rich and reliable to enable predictive maintenance.
Predictive maintenance algorithms can achieve acceptable false positive/negative rates.
The NGHE Solution module can be implemented with acceptable latency and complexity.
open_risks: Redundancy may mask underlying design flaws; root cause analysis is essential.
Predictive maintenance algorithms may have false positive/negative rates that impact availability perception.
Availability management system introduces additional complexity and potential failure modes.
Over-centralization of availability management in NGHE Solution may create bottlenecks.
Telemetry data quality issues may propagate to availability calculations.
Safety Services may suppress alerts to improve availability metrics.
evidence_needed: Detailed redundancy strategy and cost-benefit analysis for each subsystem.
Predictive maintenance algorithm design, validation data, and false positive/negative rates.
Availability monitoring system architecture, latency budget, and failure mode analysis.
Interface contracts for Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, and Network Link.
Operational test plan and field deployment plan for availability KPI validation.
Root cause analysis framework for failures and corrective action process.
